title: Increase the integer

content.problemStatement: Write a program that takes t test cases as input, then for each test case, reads an integer N, and prints N + 1.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print N+1 on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each N is increased by one: 4→5, 2→3, -1→0.

solution.approach: The task is trivial: for every test case we just need to add one to the given integer. No preprocessing or complex data structures are required. We read the number of test cases T, then loop T times, read N, compute N+1 and output it. Using BufferedReader and StringBuilder ensures fast I/O even for the maximum limits.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – each test case is processed in constant time.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) additional space besides the output buffer.

code.Java: import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();int T=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());for(int i=0;i<T;i++){int N=Integer.parseInt(br.readLine().trim());sb.append(N+1).append('\n');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
